acceptance_criteria: Notebook executes with nbclient in the repo uv environment.
Final sanity cell checks artifact existence, file size, JSON invariants, and symbolic residuals.
No source screenshots, page crops, or copied textbook prose are used.
chapter_goal: Turn the Legendre transform from a formula into an inspectable change of variables between velocities and momenta.
concept_inventory: strict convexity as positive Hessian on each tangent space
stable convex functions and the stability set S_F
Legendre transform L_F=dF and its image S_F
Young/Fenchel inequality with equality on ell=dF_v
fiberwise transform TM -> T*M and Hamiltonian H=xi v-F
coordinate proof identities converting Euler-Lagrange equations to Hamilton equations

library_routing_table.fallback: Plain numeric table of second derivatives and gradient ranges
library_routing_table.why: The chapter's first examples are one-dimensional convex functions where tangents, slopes, and runaway tilted graphs are clearest in static axes.

library_routing_table.representation: paired graph of F/F* and heatmap of F(v)+F*(ell)-ell v
library_routing_table.why: The equality set is a visible curve ell=dF_v and the residual is a nonnegative numeric array.

library_routing_table.representation: interactive Plotly surfaces for L(x,v) and H(x,xi)
library_routing_table.why: The learner can rotate the two fiber pictures and compare velocity and momentum coordinates without adding external dependencies.

library_routing_table.fallback: Markdown derivation with exact residual strings
library_routing_table.representation: symbolic identity check and dependency diagram
library_routing_table.why: The proof turns on chain-rule cancellations, and exact symbolic residuals prevent the diagram from being merely decorative.

proof_visualization_strategy: Use a dependency graph for the theorem route, then exact SymPy chain-rule residuals for the coordinate proof bridge.
risks: Plotly HTML is an external-browser artifact during static validation, so PNG companions carry the core lesson.
The PDF page map is offset for this lecture; source-span JSON records the verified physical pages.

source_span_read.physical_pdf_pages_read: 128-135 for boundary check; Lecture 20 content on 129-134
source_span_read.source_map_note: Repository map is offset here: physical page 129 begins printed page 119 / Lecture 20; physical page 135 begins Part VIII.
source_span_read.title: Legendre Transform

visual_sequence.inspection_target: which hypotheses feed the diffeomorphism and which identities feed Hamilton's equations
visual_sequence.validation: directed acyclic graph has all named lecture nodes and no isolated concept nodes

visual_sequence.inspection_target: the function exp(v)+a v is strictly convex for all a but only stable for a<0
visual_sequence.validation: sampled second derivative is positive; derivative range matches the reported stability interval

visual_sequence.inspection_target: the nonnegative gap vanishes along the graph ell=F'(v)
visual_sequence.validation: minimum sampled gap is nonnegative within tolerance and equality residual is small

visual_sequence.inspection_target: compare L(x,v) with H(x,xi) for a position-dependent mass model
visual_sequence.validation: symbolic residuals H_xi-v and H_x+F_x vanish after xi=m(x)v
